title: Effect of Bougie Use on First-Attempt Success in Tracheal Intubations: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.

1.reason: Identified as systematic review/meta-analysis in header and title area.

2.reason: Structured abstract with background, methods, results, conclusions provided.

3.reason: Background and Importance sections describe rationale in context of existing knowledge.

4.reason: Explicit objective stated in 'Goals of This Investigation'.

5.reason: Eligibility criteria section specifies inclusion/exclusion and subgroups.

6.reason: Lists databases with date last searched (June 15, 2023).

7.reason: Appendix E1 provides full search strategies.

8.reason: Two independent reviewers screened titles/abstracts and full text with consensus.

9.reason: One extractor with second reviewer cross-check; standardized form used.

10b.reason: Lists variables extracted (setting, RSI, age, sex, etc.).

11.reason: RoB 2 and modified Newcastle-Ottawa described; one assessor cross-checked.

13a.reason: Eligibility for syntheses described, including subgroups.

13b.reason: No description of handling missing summary statistics or data conversions.

13d.reason: Random-effects DerSimonian-Laird, I2, Review Manager 5.4.1 reported.

14.reason: Funnel plot used to assess publication bias.

16b.reason: Excluded studies after full-text review not individually cited with reasons.

17.reason: Table 1 presents each included study characteristics.

18.reason: Risk of bias presented in Appendix E3 and summarized in text.

19.reason: Forest plots show study-level data with effect estimates and CIs.

20a.reason: Characteristics and risk of bias of contributing studies summarized.

20b.reason: Pooled RRs, CIs, and I2 reported.

20d.reason: Sensitivity analysis excluding BOUGIE trial reported.

21.reason: Funnel plot asymmetry assessment reported in Appendix E4.

23a.reason: Discussion compares findings with prior meta-analyses.

23c.reason: Review limitations discussed (inability to do subgroup by experience, etc.).

23d.reason: Implications for practice and future research discussed.

24b.reason: Protocol available on PROSPERO; mentioned as registered there.

24c.reason: Post hoc difficult-airway subgroup analysis explicitly noted as amendment.

26.reason: Authors report no conflicts of interest.

27.reason: Data sharing statement indicates data, protocol, code available on request.

item_2.reason: States aim to evaluate evidence on bougie use and first-attempt intubation success

item_4.reason: Lists databases and search date through June 2023

item_5.reason: GRADE mentioned for certainty but no risk of bias tool specified

item_10.reason: Suggests bougie should be used first, not as rescue

title: Intubation Rates following Prehospital Administration of Ketamine for Acute Agitation: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.

9.reason: One author extracted, 10% double-checked by another; authors contacted for missing data

10a.reason: Primary outcome intubation defined; additional outcomes listed

13b.reason: Freeman-Tukey transformation, handling of pre-ketamine intubations described

title: Analgesic efficacy of nitrous oxide in adults in the emergency department: A meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.

1.reason: Title not provided in extracted text; identifies as meta-analysis in keywords but no explicit 'systematic review' label in title.

2.reason: Structured abstract with background, methods, results, conclusions provided.

3.reason: Introduction explains pain in ED, opioid concerns, and gap with no prior meta-analysis on N2O in adults.

4.reason: Explicit objectives stated: efficacy, adverse effects, satisfaction and procedure duration.

5.reason: Inclusion/exclusion criteria specified (RCTs, adults, ED/prehospital, N2O/O2 vs placebo or other).

6.reason: Six databases listed with search date August 10, 2021, plus reference list checking.

8.reason: States two authors examined articles and agreed but does not specify independent screening procedure clearly.

9.reason: Two independent authors extracted data using Excel.

10a.reason: Primary outcome (pain score VAS/NRS, pain relief) and secondary outcomes defined.

10b.reason: Lists extracted variables: author, year, sample size, age, diagnosis, N2O/O2 ratio, control method.

11.reason: Cochrane risk of bias tool used by two independent authors.

12.reason: OR for dichotomous, WMD for continuous, with 95% CI.

13a.reason: Studies grouped by comparison (placebo vs other analgesic methods).

13b.reason: VAS to NRS conversion, 0-100 to 0-10 scale, last time point selection described.

13d.reason: Fixed-effect model, random-effects if I2>50%, RevMan 5.3, I2 for heterogeneity.

13e.reason: Sensitivity/subgroup analyses by comparator type discussed.

13f.reason: Sensitivity analyses mentioned for high heterogeneity.

14.reason: No methods to assess reporting bias (e.g., funnel plot) described.

15.reason: No GRADE or certainty assessment described.

16a.reason: Flowchart Fig.1 with numbers (208+9, 26 full-text, 14 included).

16b.reason: Reasons for exclusion in S1 Appendix.

17.reason: Table 1 presents characteristics of included studies.

19.reason: Forest plots show study-level data with effect estimates and CIs.

20a.reason: Does not explicitly summarize risk of bias among contributing studies for each synthesis.

20b.reason: Pooled estimates with CIs and I2 reported for all syntheses.

20c.reason: Sensitivity/subgroup analyses for heterogeneity reported in results and limitations.

20d.reason: Sensitivity analysis results presented (e.g., N2O vs fentanyl subgroup).

23a.reason: Discussion interprets findings in context of prior literature.

23c.reason: Review process limitations not specifically discussed.

23d.reason: Implications for practice and future RCTs discussed.

24c.reason: No mention of amendments to protocol.

25.reason: Funding source given but role of funders not described.

27.reason: No statement on availability of data, code, forms beyond supplementary appendices.

item_5.reason: No risk of bias assessment method mentioned
